section_name: Exemption from Certain Examination Requirements for Licensed Nonresidents
text:
(a) The department by rule may provide that a holder of a driver’s license issued to the person by another state or Canadian province and who is otherwise qualified may, after passing the vision test and paying the required fees, be issued a driver’s license without the complete examination required under Section 521.161 (Examination of License Applicants) .

(b) A license issued under this section must be of the class of license equivalent to the license issued by the other jurisdiction.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 521. Driver's Licenses & Certificates
##### § 521.164 — Exemption from Certain Examination Requirements for Licensed Nonresidents

(a) The department by rule may provide that a holder of a driver’s license issued to the person by another state or Canadian province and who is otherwise qualified may, after passing the vision test and paying the required fees, be issued a driver’s license without the complete examination required under Section 521.161 (Examination of License Applicants) .

(b) A license issued under this section must be of the class of license equivalent to the license issued by the other jurisdiction.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*
